solution_html:
L.H.S. {tex}I + A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Now, {tex}I - A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ { - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
R.H.S. {tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ { - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha + \sin \alpha \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&{ - \sin \alpha + \cos \alpha \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ { - \cos \alpha \tan \frac{\alpha }{2} + \sin \alpha }&{\sin \alpha \tan \frac{\alpha }{2} + \cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha + \sin \alpha \frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{ - \sin \alpha + \cos \alpha \frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ { - \cos \alpha \frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}} + \sin \alpha }&{\sin \alpha \frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}} + \cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{\cos \alpha \cos \frac{\alpha }{2} + \sin \alpha \sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - \sin \alpha \cos \frac{\alpha }{2} + \cos \alpha \sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - \cos \alpha \sin \frac{\alpha }{2} + \sin \alpha \cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{\sin \alpha \sin \frac{\alpha }{2} + \cos \alpha \cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{\cos \left( {\alpha - \frac{\alpha }{2}} \right)}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - \sin \left( {\alpha - \frac{\alpha }{2}} \right)}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{\sin \left( {\alpha - \frac{\alpha }{2}} \right)}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{\cos \left( {\alpha - \frac{\alpha }{2}} \right)}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - \sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} L.H.S. = R.H.S. Proved.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 0 } & { 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and A3 - 6A2 + 7A + kI3 = 0, find the value of k.
question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 0 } & { 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and A 3 - 6A 2 + 7A + kI 3 = 0, find the value of k.
solution_html:
Given that, {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{l}} 1&0&2 \\ 0&2&1 \\ 2&0&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Now, {tex}A ^ { 2 } = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 0 } & { 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 0 } & { 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 + 0 + 4 } & { 0 + 0 + 0 } & { 2 + 0 + 6 } \\ { 0 + 0 + 2 } & { 0 + 4 + 0 } & { 0 + 2 + 3 } \\ { 2 + 0 + 6 } & { 0 + 0 + 0 } & { 4 + 0 + 9 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 5 } & { 0 } & { 8 } \\ { 2 } & { 4 } & { 5 } \\ { 8 } & { 0 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and {tex}A ^ { 3 } = A \cdot A ^ { 2 } = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 0 } & { 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 5 } & { 0 } & { 8 } \\ { 2 } & { 4 } & { 5 } \\ { 8 } & { 0 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 5 + 0 + 16 } & { 0 + 0 + 0 } & { 8 + 0 + 26 } \\ { 0 + 4 + 8 } & { 0 + 8 + 0 } & { 0 + 10 + 13 } \\ { 10 + 0 + 24 } & { 0 + 0 + 0 } & { 16 + 0 + 39 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 21 } & { 0 } & { 34 } \\ { 12 } & { 8 } & { 23 } \\ { 34 } & { 0 } & { 55 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Also we are given  that A{tex}^3{/tex} - 6A2 + 7A + kI3 = 0
{tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 21 } & { 0 } & { 34 } \\ { 12 } & { 8 } & { 23 } \\ { 34 } & { 0 } & { 55 } \end{array} \right] - 6 \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 5 } & { 0 } & { 8 } \\ { 2 } & { 4 } & { 5 } \\ { 8 } & { 0 } & { 13 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}+ 7 \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 0 } & { 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}+ k \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {21}&0&{34} \\ {12}&8&{23} \\ {34}&0&{55} \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {30}&0&{48} \\ {12}&{24}&{30} \\ {48}&0&{78} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}+ \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 7 } & { 0 } & { 14 } \\ { 0 } & { 14 } & { 7 } \\ { 14 } & { 0 } & { 21 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { k } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { k } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { k } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {21 - 30 + 7 + k}&{0 - 0 + 0 + 0}&{34 - 48 + 14 + 0} \\ {12 - 12 + 0 + 0}&{8 - 24 + 14 + k}&{23 - 30 + 7 + 0} \\ {34 - 48 + 14 + 0}&{0 - 0 + 0 + 0}&{55 - 78 + 21 + k} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 2 + k}&0&0 \\ 0&{ - 2 + k}&0 \\ 0&0&{ - 2 + k} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Therefore,on equating the corresponding elements, we get
-2 + k = 0
{tex}{/tex}Therefore, k = 2

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 5 & -1 & 4 \\ 2 & 3 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1 and use it to solve the following system of equations:
5x - y + 4z = 5
2x + 3y + 5z = 2
5x - 2y + 6z = - 1
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 5 & -1 & 4 \\ 2 & 3 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & 6 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 and use it to solve the following system of equations: 5x - y + 4z = 5 2x + 3y + 5z = 2 5x - 2y + 6z = - 1
explanation: {tex} |{A}|=51 {/tex} {tex} \text { Cofactors: } {/tex} {tex} {A}_{11}=28 {~A}_{12}=13 {~A}_{13}=-19 {/tex} {tex} {~A}_{21}=-2 {~A}_{22}=10 {~A}_{23}=5 {/tex} {tex} {~A}_{31}=-17 {~A}_{32}=-17 {~A}_{33}=17 {/tex} {tex} {A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\text { Given system is } {AX}={B} \text { where } {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right] {B}=\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-1} {~B}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} 3 \\ 2 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}{x}=3, {y}=2, {z}=-2{/tex}
solution: {tex} |{A}|=51 {/tex} {tex} \text { Cofactors: } {/tex} {tex} {A}_{11}=28 {~A}_{12}=13 {~A}_{13}=-19 {/tex} {tex} {~A}_{21}=-2 {~A}_{22}=10 {~A}_{23}=5 {/tex} {tex} {~A}_{31}=-17 {~A}_{32}=-17 {~A}_{33}=17 {/tex} {tex} {A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\text { Given system is } {AX}={B} \text { where } {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right] {B}=\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-1} {~B}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} 3 \\ 2 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}{x}=3, {y}=2, {z}=-2{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} |{A}|=51 {/tex}
{tex} \text { Cofactors: } {/tex}
{tex} {A}_{11}=28 {~A}_{12}=13 {~A}_{13}=-19 {/tex}
{tex} {~A}_{21}=-2 {~A}_{22}=10 {~A}_{23}=5 {/tex}
{tex} {~A}_{31}=-17 {~A}_{32}=-17 {~A}_{33}=17 {/tex}
{tex} {A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\text { Given system is } {AX}={B} \text { where } {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right] {B}=\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {X}={A}^{-1} {~B}=\frac{1}{51}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 28 & -2 & -17 \\ 13 & 10 & -17 \\ -19 & 5 & 17 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} 5 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} 3 \\ 2 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}{x}=3, {y}=2, {z}=-2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Express the matrix {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ { - 1}&3&4 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} as the sum of a symmetric and a skew-symmetric matrix.
question_text: Express the matrix {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ { - 1}&3&4 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} as the sum of a symmetric and a skew-symmetric matrix.
solution_html:
{tex}B' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 2}&3&{ - 2} \\ { - 4}&4&{ - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Let {tex}P = \frac{1}{2}(B + B') = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{\frac{{ - 3}}{2}}&{\frac{{ - 3}}{2}} \\ {\frac{{ - 3}}{2}}&3&1 \\ {\frac{{ - 3}}{2}}&1&{ - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}P' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{\frac{{ - 3}}{2}}&{\frac{{ - 3}}{2}} \\ {\frac{{ - 3}}{2}}&3&1 \\ {\frac{{ - 3}}{2}}&1&{ - 3} \end{array}} \right] = P{/tex}
Thus {tex}P = \frac{1}{2}(B + B'){/tex} is a symmetric matrix
Let {tex}Q = \frac{1}{3}(B - B') = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}&{\frac{{ - 5}}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{2}}&0&3 \\ {\frac{5}{2}}&{ - 3}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}Q' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}&{\frac{5}{2}} \\ {\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}&0&{ - 3} \\ {\frac{{ - 5}}{2}}&3&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}Q' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}&{\frac{{ - 5}}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{2}}&0&3 \\ {\frac{5}{2}}&{ - 3}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Q' = -Q
Thus {tex}Q = \frac{1}{2}(B - B'){/tex} is a skew symmetric matrix
{tex}P + Q = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{\frac{{ - 3}}{2}}&{\frac{{ - 3}}{2}} \\ {\frac{{ - 3}}{2}}&3&1 \\ {\frac{{ - 3}}{2}}&1&{ - 3} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{\frac{{ - 1}}{2}}&{\frac{{ - 5}}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{2}}&0&3 \\ {\frac{5}{2}}&{ - 3}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex},
Prove {tex}I + A = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
question_text: {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , Prove {tex}I + A = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
explanation: Put {tex}\tan \frac{\alpha }{2} = t{/tex} {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}I + A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}I - A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&t \\ { - t}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} L.H.S. {tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2{{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{2{{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + \frac{{t.2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \\ { - t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \frac{{2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{ - t\left( {\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2} + 2{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t + t - {t^3}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - t + {t^3} + 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{2{t^2} + 1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 + {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - {t^3} - t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{{t^3} + t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{\frac{{ - t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} L.H.S = R.H.S Hence proved
solution: Put {tex}\tan \frac{\alpha }{2} = t{/tex} {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}I + A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}I - A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&t \\ { - t}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} L.H.S. {tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2{{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{2{{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + \frac{{t.2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \\ { - t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \frac{{2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{ - t\left( {\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2} + 2{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t + t - {t^3}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - t + {t^3} + 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{2{t^2} + 1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 + {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - {t^3} - t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{{t^3} + t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{\frac{{ - t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} L.H.S = R.H.S Hence proved
solution_html:
Put {tex}\tan \frac{\alpha }{2} = t{/tex}
{tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}I + A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}I - A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&t \\ { - t}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
L.H.S. {tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2{{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{2{{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + \frac{{t.2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \\ { - t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \frac{{2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{ - t\left( {\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2} + 2{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t + t - {t^3}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - t + {t^3} + 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{2{t^2} + 1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 + {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - {t^3} - t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{{t^3} + t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{\frac{{ - t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
L.H.S = R.H.S
Hence proved

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Express the matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}as the sum of a symmetric and a skew-symmetric matrix.
question_text: Express the matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} as the sum of a symmetric and a skew-symmetric matrix.
explanation: Given, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Then A T = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 5 & -2 \\ -1 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (A + A T ) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 5 & -2 \\ -1 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right]\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4+4 & 2+3 & -1+1 \\ 3+2 & 5+5 & 7-2 \\ 1-1 & -2+7 & 1+1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 8 & 5 & 0 \\ 5 & 10 & 5 \\ 0 & 5 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X T = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ 5 & & 5 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ 5 & & 5 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = X {tex}\therefore{/tex} X is a symmetric matrix. Y = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (A - A T ) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 5 & -2 \\ -1 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right]\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4-4 & 2-3 & -1-1 \\ 3-2 & 5-5 & 7+2 \\ 1+1 & -2-7 & 1-1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -1 & -2 \\ 1 & 0 & 9 \\ 2 & -9 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Y T = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 2 & 0 & 0 \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = Y {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Y is a skew-symmetric matrix. Now, X + Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 4+0 & \frac{5}{2}-\frac{1}{2} & 0-1 \\ \frac{5}{2}+\frac{1}{2} & 5+0 & \frac{5}{2}+\frac{9}{2} \\ 0+1 & \frac{5}{2}-\frac{9}{2} & 1+0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = A Hence, X + Y = A. Thus matrix A, is expressed as sum of symmetric and skew symmetric matrices.
solution: Given, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Then A T = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 5 & -2 \\ -1 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (A + A T ) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 5 & -2 \\ -1 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right]\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4+4 & 2+3 & -1+1 \\ 3+2 & 5+5 & 7-2 \\ 1-1 & -2+7 & 1+1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 8 & 5 & 0 \\ 5 & 10 & 5 \\ 0 & 5 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X T = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ 5 & & 5 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ 5 & & 5 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = X {tex}\therefore{/tex} X is a symmetric matrix. Y = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (A - A T ) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 5 & -2 \\ -1 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right]\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4-4 & 2-3 & -1-1 \\ 3-2 & 5-5 & 7+2 \\ 1+1 & -2-7 & 1-1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -1 & -2 \\ 1 & 0 & 9 \\ 2 & -9 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Y T = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 2 & 0 & 0 \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = Y {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Y is a skew-symmetric matrix. Now, X + Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 4+0 & \frac{5}{2}-\frac{1}{2} & 0-1 \\ \frac{5}{2}+\frac{1}{2} & 5+0 & \frac{5}{2}+\frac{9}{2} \\ 0+1 & \frac{5}{2}-\frac{9}{2} & 1+0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = A Hence, X + Y = A. Thus matrix A, is expressed as sum of symmetric and skew symmetric matrices.
solution_html:
Given, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Then AT = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 5 & -2 \\ -1 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
X = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(A + AT)
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 5 & -2 \\ -1 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right]\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4+4 & 2+3 & -1+1 \\ 3+2 & 5+5 & 7-2 \\ 1-1 & -2+7 & 1+1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 8 & 5 & 0 \\ 5 & 10 & 5 \\ 0 & 5 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
XT = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ 5 & & 5 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ 5 & & 5 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}= X
{tex}\therefore{/tex} X is a symmetric matrix.
Y = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (A - AT)
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left(\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 5 & -2 \\ -1 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right]\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4-4 & 2-3 & -1-1 \\ 3-2 & 5-5 & 7+2 \\ 1+1 & -2-7 & 1-1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -1 & -2 \\ 1 & 0 & 9 \\ 2 & -9 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
YT = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 2 & 0 & 0 \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = Y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Y is a skew-symmetric matrix.
Now,
X + Y = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & \frac{5}{2} & 0 \\ \frac{5}{2} & 5 & \frac{5}{2} \\ 0 & \frac{5}{2} & 1 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\frac{1}{2} & -1 \\ \frac{1}{2} & 0 & \frac{9}{2} \\ 1 & -\frac{9}{2} & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 4+0 & \frac{5}{2}-\frac{1}{2} & 0-1 \\ \frac{5}{2}+\frac{1}{2} & 5+0 & \frac{5}{2}+\frac{9}{2} \\ 0+1 & \frac{5}{2}-\frac{9}{2} & 1+0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 5 & 7 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}= A
Hence, X + Y = A.

Thus matrix A, is expressed as sum of symmetric and skew symmetric matrices.

LHS = I + A
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
RHS {tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left( {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&0 \end{array}} \right]} \right){/tex}{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right] {/tex}
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ { - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&1 \end{array}} \right] \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha + \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}\sin \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha + \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}\cos \alpha } \\ { - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}\cos \alpha + \sin \alpha }&{\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}\sin \alpha + \cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha + \frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}\sin \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha + \frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}\cos \alpha } \\ { - \frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}\cos \alpha + \sin \alpha }&{\frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}\sin \alpha + \cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha + \frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}\sin \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha + \frac{{\sin \frac{a}{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}\cos \alpha } \\ { - \frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}\cos \alpha + \sin \alpha }&{\frac{{\sin \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}\sin \alpha + \cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{\cos \alpha \cos \frac{\alpha }{2} + \sin \frac{\alpha }{2}\sin \alpha }}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - \sin \alpha \cos \frac{\alpha }{2} + \sin \frac{\alpha }{2}\cos \alpha }}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - \cos \alpha \sin \frac{\alpha }{2} + \cos \frac{\alpha }{2}\sin \alpha }}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{\sin \alpha \sin \frac{\alpha }{2} + \cos \frac{\alpha }{2}\cos \alpha }}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{\cos \left( {\alpha - \frac{\alpha }{2}} \right)}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - \sin \left( {\alpha - \frac{\alpha }{2}} \right)}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{\sin \left( {\alpha - \frac{\alpha }{2}} \right)}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{\cos \left( {\alpha - \frac{\alpha }{2}} \right)}}{{\cos \frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&1 \end{array}} \right] = LHS{/tex}

question_html: If  {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, prove {tex}I + A = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - \tan \frac{\alpha }{2}} \\ {\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , prove {tex}I + A = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
explanation: Put {tex}\tan \frac{\alpha }{2} = t{/tex} {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}I + A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}I - A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&t \\ { - t}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} L.H.S {tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = (I -A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{2\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{2t}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + \frac{{t.2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \\ { - t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \frac{{2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{ - t\left( {\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2} + 2t^2}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t + t - {t^3}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - t + {t^3} + 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{2{t^2} + 1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 + {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - {t^3} - t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{{t^3} + t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{\frac{{ - t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Thus, L.H.S = R.H.S Hence proved

{t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{2t}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - t\left( {\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2} + 2t^2}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - {t^3} - t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{{t^3} + t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{\frac{{ - t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Thus, L.H.S = R.H.S Hence proved
solution_html:
Put  {tex}\tan \frac{\alpha }{2} = t{/tex}

{tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}I + A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}I - A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - t} \\ t&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0 \\ 0&1 \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&t \\ { - t}&0 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
L.H.S {tex} = (I - A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\cos \alpha }&{ - \sin \alpha } \\ {\sin \alpha }&{\cos \alpha } \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = (I -A)\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \\ {\frac{{2\tan \frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&t \\ { - t}&1 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{2t}}{{1 + {{\tan }^2}\frac{\alpha }{2}}}}&{\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + \frac{{t.2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}} + t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \\ { - t\left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \frac{{2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{ - t\left( {\frac{{ - 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right) + \left( {\frac{{1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \right)} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 - {t^2} + 2t^2}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - 2t + t - {t^3}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{ - t + {t^3} + 2t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{2{t^2} + 1 - {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{{1 + {t^2}}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{ - {t^3} - t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{{t^3} + t}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{\frac{{ - t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \\ {\frac{{t(1 + {t^2})}}{{1 + {t^2}}}}&{\frac{{{t^2} + 1}}{{1 + {t^2}}}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - t} \\ t&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Thus, L.H.S = R.H.S   

Hence proved
